title: not-extension-test inside allof bypasses MissingRequire check
description: TRIGGER: compile(b"if allof(not envelope :is \"from\" \"x\") { keep; }") without require [\"envelope\"]. BAD BEHAVIOR: returns Ok instead of MissingRequire(\"envelope\"). ROOT CAUSE: When check_extension_use recurses into a TestList, it calls check_extension_use on each test stmt. For stmt [Word(\"not\"), Word(\"envelope\"), ...], the first Word is \"not\", which is not in EXTENSION_TESTS. The second Word \"envelope\" is never checked: the first if-let only inspects position 0, the second if-let is guarded by w=="if"|"elsif", and the bottom for-loop only checks Form::Tag/Block/TestList items, not intermediate Word items. Same root cause as the elsif bypass: extension test names buried after a keyword (not, or in an elsif) are invisible to the checker. Confirmed empirically: is_err=false when it should be true.
